evals.name: component-test-basic
evals.prompt: Create a component test for UserService. Use @KoraAppTest to initialize the application and @TestComponent to inject the service. Test the createUser method — create a user and verify it is returned with a populated ID.
evals.expected_output: Test class with @KoraAppTest, field with @TestComponent UserService, @Test method that calls createUser and verifies the result

evals.name: component-test-mockito
evals.description: Component test with a Mockito mock
evals.prompt: Create a component test for OrderService. Use @Mock to create a mock of UserRepository and @TestComponent for OrderService. Configure the mock via when(repo.save(...)).thenReturn(...) and test the createOrder method.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Mock UserRepository, @TestComponent OrderService, when().thenReturn() setup, result verification

evals.name: component-test-mockk
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin component test for UserService. Use @MockK to mock UserRepository and @TestComponent for UserService. Configure the mock via mockk { every { repo.save(any()) } returns user } and test the createUser method.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin test with @MockK, mockk {}, every {} returns {}, result verification

evals.name: integration-test-postgres
evals.description: Integration test with PostgreSQL and Flyway
evals.prompt: Create an integration test for UserRepository using PostgreSQL with Testcontainers. Use @TestcontainersPostgreSQL for the container, @KoraAppTest for the application, and Flyway for migrations. Test the save and findById methods.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersPostgreSQL, @KoraAppTest, @TestComponent JdbcConnection, Flyway migrations, CRUD tests

evals.name: integration-test-cassandra
evals.description: Integration test with Cassandra and Scripts migrations
evals.prompt: Create an integration test for EventRepository using Cassandra with Testcontainers. Use @TestcontainersCassandra for the container, @KoraAppTest for the application, and Scripts for migrations. Test the insert and findById methods.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersCassandra, @KoraAppTest, @TestComponent CassandraConnection, Scripts migrations

evals.name: integration-test-kotlin
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin integration test for UserRepository with @TestcontainersPostgreSQL. Use JdbcConnection for SQL assertions and @TestComponent to inject the repository.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin test with @TestcontainersPostgreSQL, JdbcConnection, @TestComponent repository

evals.name: blackbox-test-appcontainer
evals.description: Black-box test with AppContainer and HttpClient
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using AppContainer. Use @Container for the container and HttpClient for HTTP requests. Test GET /api/users/{id} — verify status code 200 and the JSON response.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Container AppContainer.build(), HttpClient, HttpRequest/HttpResponse, statusCode and body verification

evals.name: blackbox-test-restassured-get
evals.description: Black-box test with RestAssured GET request
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using RestAssured. The test should make a GET request to /api/users/{id} with a pathParam and verify the JSON response via .body() assertions.
evals.expected_output: Test with RestAssured given()/when()/then(), pathParam(), body() with equalTo/notNullValue assertions

evals.name: blackbox-test-restassured-post
evals.description: Black-box test with RestAssured POST request
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using RestAssured. The test should make a POST request to /api/users with a JSON body (name, email) and verify the response: status 201, Location header, ID in the response.
evals.expected_output: Test with RestAssured, contentType(JSON), body() with Map/POJO, statusCode(201) and header(Location) verification

evals.name: blackbox-test-telemetry
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using AppContainer with two ports (8080 API, 8085 system). Verify the /health endpoint on the system port via getSystemURI() and the /metrics endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Test with AppContainer, getSystemURI(), /health and /metrics endpoint verification, status 200

evals.name: config-modifier-system-property
evals.prompt: Create a test using KoraAppTestConfigModifier to set the system property 'external.api.url'. Use @KoraAppTest(modifiers = [KoraAppTestConfigModifier.ofSystemProperty(...)]).
evals.expected_output: Test with @KoraAppTest(modifiers = ...), KoraAppTestConfigModifier.ofSystemProperty()

evals.name: config-modifier-resource-file
evals.prompt: Create a test using KoraConfigModification.ofResourceFile to load config from application-test.conf. Use it in @KoraAppTest(modifiers = [...]).
evals.expected_output: Test with KoraConfigModification.ofResourceFile("application-test.conf")

evals.name: config-modifier-inline-string
evals.prompt: Create a test using KoraConfigModification.ofString for inline configuration of database.url and database.password. Pass a HOCON string in @KoraAppTest(modifiers = [...]).
evals.expected_output: Test with KoraConfigModification.ofString() containing database.url and database.password in HOCON format

evals.name: graph-modifier-add-component
evals.description: Adding a component to the graph
evals.prompt: Create a test using KoraAppTestGraphModifier to add a component to the dependency graph. Use the .add() method to register a test component.
evals.expected_output: Test with KoraAppTestGraphModifier.add() to add a component

evals.name: graph-modifier-remove-component
evals.description: Removing a component from the graph
evals.prompt: Create a test using KoraAppTestGraphModifier to remove a component from the graph. Use the .remove() method to exclude a component from the DI graph.
evals.expected_output: Test with KoraAppTestGraphModifier.remove() to remove a component

evals.name: tag-injection
evals.prompt: Create a test using the @Tag annotation to inject a specific bean. Use @TestComponent and @Tag("primary") to select the required interface implementation.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Tag("primary") and @TestComponent for qualifier injection

evals.name: spy-test
evals.prompt: Create a test using @Spy to create a spy of a real component. Verify the method call via verify(spy).method() from Mockito.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Spy, verify() assertion from Mockito

evals.name: constructor-injection
evals.description: Constructor injection instead of field injection
evals.prompt: Create a test using constructor injection with @TestComponent instead of field injection. The class constructor should accept dependencies as parameters annotated with @TestComponent.
evals.expected_output: Test class with a constructor accepting @TestComponent parameters

evals.name: method-injection
evals.prompt: Create a test using method parameter injection. The test method should accept a @TestComponent parameter directly in its method signature.
evals.expected_output: @Test method with a @TestComponent parameter in the signature

evals.name: mockito-strictness
evals.prompt: Create a test with @MockitoSettings(strictness = Strictness.STRICT_STUBS) for strict stubbing verification. All stubs must be used in the test.
evals.expected_output: Test with @MockitoSettings(strictness = Strictness.STRICT_STUBS) on the class or method

evals.name: mock-argument-captor
evals.prompt: Create a test using ArgumentCaptor to capture arguments of a mock method. Use argumentCaptor.capture() and getValue()/getAllValues() for verification.
evals.expected_output: Test with ArgumentCaptor<T>, capture(), getValue() or getAllValues()

evals.name: shared-network
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using @Network(shared = true) for a shared network between AppContainer and PostgreSQLContainer. This allows the application to connect to the DB inside the Docker network.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Network(shared = true), AppContainer, PostgreSQLContainer on the same network

evals.name: per-class-lifecycle
evals.prompt: Create a test with @TestInstance(TestInstance.Lifecycle.PER_CLASS) for single container initialization. This allows containers to be reused between tests in the class.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestInstance(Lifecycle.PER_CLASS) on the class

evals.name: kafka-consumer-test
evals.prompt: Create a test for a Kafka consumer using @TestcontainersKafka and Awaitility for async assertions. Send a message to Kafka and verify that the consumer processed it.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersKafka, @ConnectionKafka, KafkaConnection.send(), Awaitility.await()

evals.name: kafka-producer-test
evals.prompt: Create a test for a Kafka producer using @TestcontainersKafka. Subscribe to the topic via connection.subscribe(), send a message via the producer, and verify via assertReceivedAtLeast().
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersKafka, subscribe(), producer.send(), assertReceivedAtLeast()

evals.name: mockserver-test
evals.prompt: Create a test using MockServer to mock an external HTTP API. Use @TestcontainersMockServer and MockServerClient to configure expectations (when/withResponse).
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersMockServer, MockServerClient, when(request()).withResponse()

evals.name: blackbox-test-kotlin-restassured
evals.prompt: Create a Kotlin black-box test using RestAssured. The test should make a GET request to /api/users/{id} and verify the JSON response via body {} assertions.
evals.expected_output: Kotlin test with RestAssured, given/when/then, pathParam, body {} assertions

evals.name: component-test-verify-mock
evals.prompt: Create a component test that verifies a mock method was called. Use @Mock, @TestComponent, and verify(mock, times(N)).method() to verify the calls.
evals.expected_output: Test with @Mock, verify(mock, times(N)) or verify(mock) assertions

evals.name: integration-test-liquibase
evals.prompt: Create an integration test for UserRepository using PostgreSQL with Liquibase migrations instead of Flyway. Use @TestcontainersPostgreSQL and Liquibase for migrations.
evals.expected_output: Test with @TestcontainersPostgreSQL, Liquibase, JdbcConnection, CRUD tests

evals.name: blackbox-test-json-schema
evals.description: Black-box test with JSON Schema validation
evals.prompt: Create a black-box test using RestAssured with JSON Schema validation. Verify that the response matches the JSON schema via jsonSchemaValidator().
evals.expected_output: Test with RestAssured, jsonSchemaValidator(), JSON response schema

evals.name: test-application-basic
evals.prompt: Create a TestApplication that extends Application and adds TestPetRepository with findAll() and deleteAll() methods for cleaning the pets table in tests. Include instructions for configuring the submodule in build.gradle.
evals.expected_output: TestApplication interface extends Application, TestPetRepository with @Query for SELECT and DELETE, submodule.enabled instruction

evals.name: test-application-with-usage
evals.prompt: Create a TestApplication with TestPetRepository and a test that uses @KoraAppTest(TestApplication.class) and calls deleteAll() in @BeforeEach. Include instructions for configuring the submodule.
evals.expected_output: TestApplication interface and test class with @BeforeEach calling deleteAll(), submodule.enabled instruction

evals.name: test-application-generic-component
evals.prompt: Create a TestApplication that adds a test component for an external service (not a DB). Add FakeExternalService as a @Root @Component default method. Include instructions for configuring the submodule.
evals.expected_output: TestApplication with a default method returning FakeExternalService, @Root @Component, submodule instruction
